title: FDAI chaos scenario
description: Catalog-as-code shape for one fault-injection scenario, mirroring the upstream `fdai.core.chaos.contract.FaultScenario` dataclass plus provenance / gate progress / domain fields. Every YAML file under `rule-catalog/chaos-scenarios/{collected,promoted}/**` and `rule-catalog/chaos-scenarios-custom/**` MUST validate against this schema.

id.description: Dotted namespace + kebab id. Namespace is the domain (e.g. `chaos.aks.pod-kill`, `chaos.chaos-mesh.pod-failure`, `chaos.gpu.driver-xid-74`). Each dot-part is one or more lowercase alphanumeric groups joined by `-` or `_` separators. Grep-friendly, audit-safe.

version.description: Monotonic version. Bump on any breaking parameter change.

scope.description: Perturbation scope; optional (defaults to `one` at load time).

duration_seconds.description: Time-in-fault hold. Upstream reference scenarios use 360s (5-minute alert window + probe cycle); collected/synthesized may use shorter for shadow probes.

expected_signal.description: Must be a registered SIGNAL_* in `src/fdai/core/detection/signals.py`. Loader rejects unknown signals.

injector.description: Reference to the injector implementation. Format: `<family>:<subtype>` where family is lowercase-kebab and subtype MAY be CamelCase (e.g. Chaos Mesh CRD kinds `chaos-mesh:StressChaos`, `chaos-mesh:PodChaos`) or lowercase-kebab (`kubectl:pod-kill`, `az:vm-run-command`). Two opt-out markers: `needs-injector` (delivery adapter has not landed yet) and `cross-csp-reference` (catalog data borrowed from another CSP for symptom vocabulary + RCA; never executed here). Both markers are non-executable and rejected in `promoted/`.

params.description: Injector-specific parameters. Values are stringifiable primitives so the scenario stays audit-log serializable.

target_selector.description: CSP-neutral `<type>:<name>` opaque handle. Optional in the catalog (may be supplied by the caller at run time), required by the harness.

first_measured_ttfo_ms.description: Time-to-first-observed in ms from `scripts/measure-detection-latency.py`; null until measured.

requires_hardware.description: True if the scenario cannot be validated in enforce mode without dedicated hardware (e.g. a GPU VM). Such scenarios MAY only carry `enforce_status: pending` until a hardware substrate is available.
